commit.message:
v0.3.24: stability — single-flight optimize, SQLite busy_timeout, transactional savepoints

- Single-flight optimize guard: rejects concurrent optimize with HTTP 409
- SQLite busy_timeout 5s to prevent 'database is locked' under contention
- Explicit SAVEPOINT/ROLLBACK for pack merge, rename, stub merge, orphan
  repair, and dedup cleanup in optimizer
- Factory reset uses savepoint + finally block for guaranteed FK restoration
- Optimize task failures now logged with full traceback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix copilot provider bugs from PR #61 review

- fix isIdeHook to use || instead of ?? so hooks with marker in bash/powershell fields are detected correctly
- add setupCopilotWatchers branch in config-watcher.ts (was falling through to Claude watchers)
- use pythonBin from platform.ts in captureSessionIdCopilotCmd (fixes Linux/macOS)
- pass STATUS_DIR as CLI arg instead of baking into Python string literal (fixes paths with special chars)
- remove unnecessary async from getCopilotConfig; wrap in Promise.resolve() at call site
- add copilot-hooks.test.ts with 18 tests covering install/validate/cleanup and the isIdeHook bug
- add TODO comment on STATUS_EVENTS to verify event names against Copilot CLI docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* add github copilot session tab icon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix copilot provider hooks to match cli docs

Copilot CLI loads hooks from <cwd>/.github/hooks/*.json, not
~/.copilot/config.json. The prior layout, schema, and payload
assumptions all contradicted the official docs, so hooks never ran —
the inspector timeline was empty and session state never transitioned.

Rewrites hook install to write <cwd>/.github/hooks/vibeyard-copilot-hooks.json
with the documented {version:1, hooks:{...}} schema and per-entry
bash+powershell fields. Adds a dispatcher script that writes .status
and .events keyed off VIBEYARD_SESSION_ID. Covers all seven Copilot
events (sessionStart, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse, postToolUse,
errorOccurred, agentStop, sessionEnd). Installs hooks per-cwd at
spawnPty time since config is project-scoped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update gitignore to exclude vibeyard-copilot-hooks.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix copilot hooks warning on session spawn

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add copilot agents and skills config reading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Money safety (P1):

  hooks that get overwritten per component
- M-9: Fix useSiteInfo — null guard on API response with spread merge
- M-11: Dashboard now includes recent_orders + fix field name mismatch
  (amount→total_amount, today_revenue→today_income)

i18n: Add query_password_placeholder and enter_password_required keys
for all 3 locales (en, zh-CN, zh-TW)

Python (5):

Cloud/Platform (15):
- cloud-aws-deployment.md — Lambda, ECS, S3, RDS, IAM, OIDC CI/CD
- cloud-gcp-deployment.md — Cloud Run, GCS, Cloud SQL, Workload Identity

- integration-graphql.md — schema-first SDL, DataLoader, Relay pagination

commit.message:
core: fix market-status early-close calendar semantics

The early_close_holidays check was dead code — the holiday return
fired before it could run.  The early_close_eves config used holiday
names that didn't match the holidays library output (e.g.
'Christmas Eve' vs 'Christmas Day').

Fix the control flow: determine early-close status BEFORE the holiday
return so same-day half-holidays are treated as early close, not full
closure.  Add early_close_day_after field for day-after-holiday half
days (Black Friday).  Fix HKEX and ASX configs to match against the
actual holiday name the library produces.

Includes tests for same-day, day-after, and eve rule types plus
upcoming holiday impact metadata.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): point release-please to crate path, not workspace root

release-please always fetches root Cargo.toml and tries to parse it
as a Rust package manifest. By setting the package path to
'crates/convergio-types' instead of '.', it only looks at the crate
Cargo.toml which has [package].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: create PR for fixture data instead of pushing to main

The collect job was pushing fixture data directly to main, which violates
the branch protection rules requiring PRs and status checks.

Replace the direct git push with peter-evans/create-pull-request to open
a PR for the data changes, then enable auto-merge so it merges once checks
pass. Pages is still built and deployed in the same run using the freshly
collected data from the workspace.

commit.message:
patterns: add request-scoped pivot and feature caches to ElliottWaveAnalyzer

Add _pivot_cache and _wave_feature_cache dicts to ElliottWaveAnalyzer
so that pivot computation (zigzag + min-distance enforcement) and wave
feature extraction are done once per (threshold, min_distance) pair
across the autotune grid.  The autotune loop now calls
analyzer.pivot_signature() instead of the standalone
_pivot_signature_for_settings(), eliminating duplicate pivot
computation per grid point.

commit.message:
feat: add progress feedback for large collection fetching (#21)

Closes #20
Large collections (hundreds of items) produce no output while resolving
external IDs per-item via HTTP, making the tool appear hung.

### Changes

- **`plex.Client.OnProgress` callback** — called with `(current, total)`
after each item is processed in `GetCollectionItems`, where the per-item
metadata lookups are the bottleneck
- **High-level status in `resolveCollection`** — prints
finding/fetching/loaded messages to stdout so the user sees activity
immediately
- **Stderr progress line in `main()`** — `newCollectionProgressFunc`
writes `\rProcessing collection items: N/M` with in-place
carriage-return updates; newline on completion

```
Finding collection "Movies"...
Fetching items for "Movies"...
Processing collection items: 142/200   ← stderr, in-place
Loaded 200 items from "Movies"
```

The callback is nil-safe — no behavior change when unset (e.g., in tests
or the bridge package).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nipuna-perera <11575062+nipuna-perera@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Publish v2: add mariadb importer (#11)

* feat: add SecForm4MariadbInserter node and MySqlCredentials

Add a new n8n node that takes parsed SEC Form 4 data and upserts it
into a MySQL/MariaDB database. Features:

- MySqlCredentials credential type for database connection
- Idempotent upserts using INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
- Automatic table creation (10 normalized tables) via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
- Transactional writes with rollback on error
- Configurable table prefix (default: sec_)
- 18-test suite covering SQL generation, transactions, and rollback

commit.message:
Update laststanding: Linux TLS via BearSSL amalgamation

Update submodule to pick up Linux TLS support (BearSSL). Embed
bearssl_amalg.c (~991KB) alongside l_tls.h so freestanding builds
on Linux get TLS automatically. No Oscan code changes needed —
the existing l_tls_* wrappers now call real implementations on Linux
instead of stubs returning -1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deep testing: 37 new tests, 4 bug fixes

Bugs fixed (found by GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.3-Codex stress testing):

- MatterStore.list(limit=0) returned all entities instead of none;
  now correctly honors zero and clamps negative limits
- compute_deadline parsed YYYY-MM-DD dates in UTC, causing off-by-one
  errors on holiday/weekend boundaries; fixed with local date parsing
- generate_invoice accepted zero/negative hours without warning;
  now skips invalid entries and returns warnings array
- process_document reported lineCount=1 for empty content; fixed to 0

New test files:
- tests/agent/stress-scenarios.test.ts (29 tests): MatterStore
  isolation across 100 entities and 5 matters, unicode/special chars,
  entity update semantics, deletion + link cleanup, search relevance,
  linking chains and edge cases, tool execution errors, system prompt
  verification, conflict checker, chronology engine edge cases
- tests/agent/product-quality.test.ts (8 tests): full litigation
  lifecycle, concurrent operations, privilege marker handling, real
  FRCP deadline scenarios, document processing pipeline, invoice
  arithmetic accuracy, cross-module linking, search relevance

521 tests total, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Caddy checksum verification: use SHA-512 to match upstream checksums

Caddy's checksums.txt uses SHA-512 (128 hex chars), but the verification
code expected SHA-256 (64 hex chars). This caused caddyChecksumFromFile
to skip valid hash entries and fail with 'failed to find sha256 checksum'.

Changes:
- Switch from crypto/sha256 to crypto/sha512
- Update hash length check from 64 to 128
- Rename sha256File to sha512File
- Update test to verify SHA-512 behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(bot): extract BFS crawler with Fetcher abstraction

Decouple BFS traversal logic from HTTP fetching to enable unit testing.

**Changes:**
- Add crawler package with Fetcher interface
  - FileFetcher for file-based testing (no network required)
  - HTTPFetcher for production HTTP requests
- Implement complete BFS with FIFO queue
- Add URL utilities (normalize, domain validation, extension filtering)
- Add HTML link extraction with golang.org/x/net/html
- Create 17 unit tests with 84.2% coverage
- Update bot/README.md with crawler usage examples

**Architecture:**
- Dependency inversion: BFSCrawler depends on Fetcher interface
- Strategy pattern: swap FileFetcher/HTTPFetcher without changing crawler
- Context-aware: all operations support cancellation

**Testing:**
- All tests pass using FileFetcher with HTML fixtures
- No network required for testing
- Tests verify: depth limits, domain filtering, BFS order, deduplication

**OpenSpec:**
- Modified bot-pioneer-crawler spec (added Fetcher scenarios)
- Archived change: openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-12-simple-bfs-crawler
- Pioneer integration deferred (4 tasks remaining)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add WhisperX speaker diarization support

- Add whisperx_transcribe.py script for speaker diarization
- Add TranscribeWithDiarizationAsync to WhisperService
- Update /transcribe endpoint to accept diarize parameter
- Update /health endpoint with diarizationAvailable field
- Add WhisperX setup to installer with HuggingFace token prompt
- Add DiarizationTimeoutSeconds and HuggingFaceToken config
- Update README with diarization documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: OpenScanner.WhisperServer
repository.description: Remote Whisper server for OpenScanner
